synthesis.summary: Segurobet partner contact Danilo Tostes reported via Intercom (bot-classified P1) that SMS sends for CRM Activity 3186 weren't firing; a clone attempt (3192) also failed. Root cause was the Segurobet 'am-fire' notification instance having been downsized (an EV from 8 Jul) combined with a misconfigured prefetch count that kept the KEDA scaledobject from scaling — the same failure mode recurred earlier that same day at 05:19 CEST. Matteo Rapisarda (Incident Lead) diagnosed and fixed it directly with the partner in Intercom, fully drained both queues, and the partner confirmed satisfaction less than 90 minutes after reporting.

The gap since Friday afternoon is a weekend lull, not a stall.

synthesis.summary: Cocobet (Mystake) blocked users appear to 'unblock' themselves because the partner's daily recon feed omits users who should stay blocked. Joachim confirmed the exact mechanism and sent the partner a clear technical ask early on (12:17-12:19 CEST); the partner then went silent all day — confirmed unresponsive by Giancarlo at ~19:07 CEST. Rather than keep waiting, Giancarlo applied an internal mitigation (~19:09-19:47 CEST): resynced Cocobet's origin and blacklisted it from the daily recon job (~20k players on that origin), then handed the incident to Matteo Rapisarda at shift change. Status remains 'Waiting on Partner' with zero partner response since the initial ask; the incident.io record's own update text still doesn't mention the mitigation or handover.
key_events: 17 Jul 10:17 UTC (12:17 CEST) Joachim confirms exact mechanism with 2 specific blocked users and sends partner a technical ask
17 Jul 13:34 CEST Joachim: 'No feedback as of yet from the partner on this'
17 Jul 19:06-19:08 CEST Andrea asks 'any traction on this?'; Giancarlo confirms partner unresponsive since ~14:00 CEST re-ping, likely signed off
17 Jul 19:09-19:47 CEST Giancarlo runs a user migration to resync Cocobet's origin and blacklists it from daily recon; confirms done ~19:47 CEST, ~20k players on that origin
17 Jul 19:10 CEST handover to Matteo Rapisarda for the 19:30 CEST shift change
17 Jul 20:05 CEST incident.io bot prompts for a status update — none posted since; incident.io record unchanged since the 10:31 UTC update

promises: root-cause explanation and recon-feed check request — delivered 12:18-12:19 CEST; no partner reply since, on Intercom or Slack, through at least 20:05 CEST 17 Jul

proposals: Resync affected origin via user migration and blacklist Cocobet's origin from the daily recon job — applied and confirmed by Giancarlo ~19:47 CEST 17 Jul (~20k players on that origin)
open_breaches: C9/C10: partner has not replied since being given the root-cause explanation/ask at 12:18 CEST 17 Jul — silence now confirmed overnight via the 07:05 CEST 18 Jul SOC handover ('no feedback from partner as of yet'), ~19h after the ask; for a True P1, this now warrants an active chase (account/partner manager), not further passive waiting

D15: incident.io record has not been refreshed since the 10:31 UTC update — it still omits the internal mitigation applied ~19:09-19:47 CEST (origin resync + recon blacklist) and the shift handover to Matteo Rapisarda, now going on ~19h stale
synthesis.why: True P1, 'Waiting on Partner' for ~19h+ as of the 18 Jul morning run (no new Slack/Intercom activity overnight; SOC handover explicitly confirms the partner is still silent), incident.io record still doesn't reflect the applied mitigation or current owner (D15). Handling itself is competent (precise root cause, proactive internal workaround instead of just waiting) but partner silence approaching a full day on a True P1 now warrants an active chase.
synthesis.suggested_action: Have Joachim (Incident Lead) or the current owner (Matteo Rapisarda) escalate to Cocobet's account/partner manager to force a response on fixing the recon feed, and post an incident.io update capturing the applied mitigation (origin resync + recon blacklist) and current owner so the record is self-contained.

synthesis.summary: Meridianbet-wide delayed message processing across CRM integration, Singularity, kafka-shovel and RabbitMQ, absorbing 12+ merged alerts/incidents. Joachim took lead instantly, scaled up Singularity, and by 15:31 CEST 7 attached alerts had resolved; status moved to Monitoring. Lead handed to Giancarlo at 16:34 CEST as more alerts kept attaching and resolving through 16:40 CEST. Root cause itself is still recorded as 'Unknown' in incident.io despite the mitigation working.
key_events: 17 Jul 12:53 UTC alert fires, 8 alerts attached within the hour, Joachim leads
17 Jul 15:03 CEST Joachim: kafka lag on consumer now processing (after scaling Singularity)
17 Jul 15:30 CEST Andrea updates status to Monitoring; 7 attached alerts resolved
17 Jul 16:34 CEST lead handed to Giancarlo; more alerts still merging/resolving through 16:40 CEST
proposals: Scale up Singularity - applied, working
open_breaches: D15: incident.io 'Causes' field still 'Unknown' despite substantial mitigation already applied
synthesis.why: Fast, competent scaling response containing a wide multi-service alert storm, but the record doesn't yet explain what actually caused the migration/sendout backlog. 18 Jul run: a 01:42 CEST bot nudge ('no activity, can this be resolved?') went unanswered overnight, and the 07:05 CEST SOC handover flags it again ('no alerts triggered during night shift, to be confirmed by SRE if this incident could be resolved') — quiet and stable, just needs someone to confirm closure or add the root cause.
synthesis.suggested_action: Whoever holds the lead (Giancarlo) to add the root cause once identified, not just the mitigation steps.

synthesis.summary: ElastiCache memory-utilization alert for production-304-gen (aws-elasticache) fired at 03:44 UTC (05:44 CEST) 18 Jul; auto-escalation paged Gérard E. Pelayo, who acknowledged within a minute and was set as Incident Lead. First working update came ~77min later (07:01 CEST) confirming the instance is already at cache.r6g.4xlarge with memory holding ~90-91%, so monitoring rather than upgrading further. Joachim Farrugia then joined and ran a Redis key-cleaning pass on Galabet at 07:12 CEST, moving status Triage → Fixing.
key_events: 18 Jul 03:44 UTC alert fires, incident auto-created P2
18 Jul 05:44 CEST Gérard E. Pelayo acknowledges, set as Incident Lead
18 Jul 07:01 CEST Gérard reports memory holding ~90-91% on cache.r6g.4xlarge, monitoring rather than upgrading
18 Jul 07:12 CEST Joachim Farrugia runs Redis key cleaning on Galabet; status moved Triage → Fixing
proposals: Monitor at current instance size (cache.r6g.4xlarge); upgrade only if necessary — in progress
Redis key cleaning on Galabet — applied 07:12 CEST 18 Jul
synthesis.why: Fresh P2 infra alert with prompt escalation-ack and lead assigned instantly; first working update landed within normal grace (~77min) and status is progressing (Triage→Fixing). Also flagged in the 07:05 CEST SOC handover with an existing owner. No breach evident — flagged for deep-read only because P2 severity always warrants a look.
